# TrimEditor

> TrimEditor is a Kotlin Paper plugin by Jan1k (GitHub: Jan1k1) for editing Minecraft armor trims on the item a player is holding.

TrimEditor targets Paper and Folia servers on Minecraft 1.21+. It provides a GUI flow, optional item/economy costs, safe held-item validation, and a public API for other plugins such as KiteEditor.

## Core Documentation

- [Home](https://jan1k1.github.io/TrimEditor/): Overview, status, compatibility, and quick start.
- [Installation](https://jan1k1.github.io/TrimEditor/installation/): Server install steps and runtime requirements.
- [Commands](https://jan1k1.github.io/TrimEditor/commands/): `/te`, `/trimeditor`, and reload behavior.
- [Configuration](https://jan1k1.github.io/TrimEditor/configuration/): Config versioning, requirements, economy, sounds, language, and safety notes.
- [Permissions](https://jan1k1.github.io/TrimEditor/permissions/): Permission nodes for players, admins, trim patterns, trim materials, and bypasses.
- [API](https://jan1k1.github.io/TrimEditor/api/): Public API usage for plugins such as KiteEditor.
- [Building](https://jan1k1.github.io/TrimEditor/building/): Gradle tasks, project layout, and local server testing.

## Source

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/Jan1k1/TrimEditor): Source code, issues, commits, and CI.
- [README](https://github.com/Jan1k1/TrimEditor/blob/main/README.md): Short project overview.

## Maintainer

- Creator and maintainer: Jan1k / Jan1k1.
- Portfolio: https://jan1k.org/
